conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Evaluating project management tools for the team -- Jira, Linear, and Asana are the finalists.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Jira has the most integrations but it's always felt kind of heavy for a small team.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Asana's timeline view looked nice in the demo, but pricing per seat adds up with our headcount.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to get a new charger, mine finally died.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Linear's snappy, mostly keyboard-based interface keeps coming up when I ask other small teams what they use.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Got a new charger, cheaper brand this time, hope it lasts longer than the last one.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Trialed Linear for a week and the responsiveness compared to Jira made switching back annoying.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Still not fully sold since migrating our existing Jira boards would take some effort.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My internet provider called about a plan change, might switch providers.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Estimated the Jira migration would only take about a day given how few projects we actually have active.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We're moving the team to Linear. The keyboard-driven workflow and how fast it loads made the migration effort worth it over sticking with Jira.

query: Which project management tool did the team decide to switch to?
